kind: dense data-model fast path with dense output for BroadcastInDim
lever_summary: BroadcastInDim replicated input into a full-size Vec<Literal>. Added a dense fast path: dense source (F64/I64/Bool slice) replicated via a generic-over-T row-major index kernel (broadcast_replicate) directly into new_*_values dense storage, bypassing input materialization + the Vec<Literal> output. Generic path now also shares broadcast_replicate.
method: same-bench A/B; baseline = stashed generic Vec<Literal> broadcast; normalized by eval/sort_calib_reduce_64k_i64 (ReduceSum, unaffected).

change.lever: Factored broadcast_replicate<T: Copy>; eval_broadcast_in_dim routes dense F64/I64/Bool sources to new_*_values output, generic for the rest. Added eval/broadcast_256_to_256x256_f64 bench + broadcast_in_dim_dense_matches_literal_path test.

isomorphism.type: BIT-IDENTICAL: broadcast_replicate implements identical row-major odometer + in_flat mapping for dense and Literal element types; dense output value-equal to Literal-backed

validation.name: broadcast_tests
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --release broadcast_in_dim

validation.name: clippy
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --release --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings

note: 2.79x (vs Convert's 14.8x) because the per-element odometer remains the dominant cost; the win is removing input materialization + Vec<Literal> output. Dense vein now covers reductions/cumulative/argmax/elementwise/convert/broadcast.
